text: Magic is not given. It is traded. The shrine demands a token of life to come back the
call of death. Kale does not hesitate. She presses her hand to the shard, a covenant
written in blood, and the mountain answers. The rooms silent for an age drink the offering
and awaken. A cool blue light pulses from stone to stone, a heartbeat returning to a
dead thing. It is the light of life itself, drawn from the warmth of her own vein.
